_comment: LI.FI Diamond is the universal cross-chain routing layer on Pharos. Use it for (a) non-USDC tokens, (b) cross-chain swaps (e.g. PROS->USDC@Base) atomically via LI.FI Intents in seconds, (c) routes CCTP doesn't cover. For plain USDC<->USDC bridges, prefer CCTP V2 in cctp-domains.json (zero fee).

pharos._note: LI.FI Diamond proxy. Quote responses' transactionRequest.to points here.

pharos_token_registry._comment: Tokens LI.FI knows about on Pharos chain 1672. Use SYMBOL in /quote (LI.FI resolves to address). Address overlaps with Circle USDC for USDC; others are LI.FI-specific bridged variants.

WETH._note: LI.FI's WETH on Pharos, distinct from the tokens.json WETH

WPROS._note: LI.FI's WPROS, distinct from WPHRS in tokens.json

bridges_on_pharos._comment: Bridge providers LI.FI orchestrates from/to Pharos. CCTP V2 is NOT among these — LI.FI uses generic message-passing protocols. Use this list to set 'allowBridges' / 'denyBridges' params on /quote if you want to force a route.

exchanges_on_pharos._comment: Same-chain DEX aggregators LI.FI can route through on Pharos.

status_values.NOT_FOUND: tx not yet detected by LI.FI indexer

DONE.PARTIAL: success but received different token (refunded mid-flight)

status_values.FAILED: transaction failed, may need user action

polling._note: LI.FI Intents typically settle in <60 sec. Polymer Standard ~18 min. Use executionDuration from quote as expected time.

preferred_route_policy._comment: Decision policy (v0.3.0 — LI.FI-first). The agent ALWAYS queries LI.FI first, then optionally quotes CCTP or Faroswap as alternatives when the intent type allows it. Best route is picked at runtime by ranking on (speed, fee, output).

specialized_alternatives.USDC_to_USDC_with_pharos: ALSO quote CCTP V2 (often zero fee but 8-15 min — compare vs LI.FI Polymer ~18 min, 0.25%)
specialized_alternatives.same_chain_pharos_swap: ALSO quote Faroswap (DODO Route API or direct mixSwap)

preferred_route_policy.stuck_route_fallback: If LI.FI returns no route, try CCTP V2 (USDC pairs) or report unsupported corridor

pharos_pros_supported_corridors._comment: Manually verified 2026-06-16 — PROS bidirectional with LI.FI on Pharos. NOT exhaustive — always re-verify via live /quote. Use as a fast hint for the agent when discussing 'what can I do with my PROS'.
pharos_pros_supported_corridors._method: Confirmed via LI.FI /quote returning a valid transactionRequest in both directions.
_unsupported_chains_for_PROS: avalanche (no LI.FI PROS route as of 2026-06-16)
